title: Hoist torch_npu imports and enforce NPU-only device selection
replacement:
import torch
import torch_npu

npu = getattr(torch, "npu", None)
if npu is None or not npu.is_available():
    raise RuntimeError("Ascend NPU is required; CPU fallback is forbidden")
DEVICE = "npu:0"

postconditions: torch_npu is imported at module scope adjacent to import torch
NPU availability is checked with getattr(torch, "npu", None) and npu.is_available()
RuntimeError is raised when Ascend NPU is unavailable
Runtime device is set to npu:0 instead of CUDA or CPU fallback
antipatterns: Do not inject import torch_npu inside except blocks, functions, loops, or other nested scopes
Do not preserve CPU fallback behavior after NPU migration
Do not keep CUDA device selection in validation entrypoints
validation: Run Python syntax validation after rewrite to catch misplaced imports or indentation errors
Run the migrated validation command and require it to fail fast if torch.npu is unavailable

positive_signals: import torch with injected import torch_npu
torch_npu import appears in nested or exception-handler scope
IndentationError or syntax failure after import injection
device-selection logic contains CPU or CUDA fallback
validation entrypoint must run only on Ascend NPU
required_rule_actions: hoist import torch_npu to module-level imports near import torch
insert immediate torch.npu availability gate after imports
raise RuntimeError when NPU is unavailable
set active runtime device to npu:0
rerun syntax/import validation and migrated runtime command
anti_patterns: placing backend imports inside except blocks or interactive loops
preserving CUDA device selection after NPU migration

classifier.reasoning: The candidate is a mechanical migration rule, not an agent procedure or case narrative: injected torch_npu imports should be placed at module scope near import torch, rewritten entrypoints should fail fast when torch.npu is unavailable, and migrated scripts should select npu:0 instead of retaining CUDA or CPU fallback behavior. The evidence supports a reusable post-rewrite invariant, though confidence is slightly reduced because several referenced artifacts were unavailable.

set active runtime device to npu:0

problem_description: A migrated import landed inside an exception block and made the validation entry syntactically invalid before NPU runtime execution. The repaired scripts also removed CUDA/CPU fallback behavior by requiring torch.npu availability and setting the active device to npu:0.
rough_fix_approach: 1. Move injected import torch_npu statements to module-level imports near import torch. 2. Add a torch.npu availability check immediately after imports. 3. Raise RuntimeError if NPU is unavailable instead of falling back to CPU. 4. Set the runtime device explicitly to npu:0 and rerun the validation command.
